title.rendered: Our Top Five Tips for Planning a Trip to Australia

content.rendered:
Australia is on most people’s holiday wish list. But where do you start when planning a trip to such a vast and diverse country? Here are our top five tips for planning a trip to Australia…

  1. It is important to decide what type of holiday you want. Sightseeing? City hopping? Sports and adventure? Wine tasting? Sailing? Enjoying exquisite beaches? You are not going to be able to fit it all in if you are taking a two to three week holiday, but if you can decide in advance how you want to spend the time, then it is going to much easier to plan and decide the best parts of Australia to discover.
  2. Choose the time of year carefully. For example, in Queensland the summers are extremely hot, humid and rainy. October or November in Spring is a great time to head down under and discover Queensland. In fact, it is a great time of the year to discover any parts of Australia.
  3. Jet lag going in the direction of Australia is worse then coming back to the UK, so it is worth considering how you can minimise the impact. A stopover for a day or two in Asia is a good way to break up the trip. Depending on the airline you are using, it might be worth stopping where they stop. A couple of days in Singapore is a great way to cram more into your trip and help to manage jetlag. Or, if you really want to maximise your trip, you could stop at Perth before heading to the east coast. It won’t really help with jetlag, but it is a way to break up the journey. You don’t realise until you are watching the tiny plane on the TV screens tracking your journey just how vast Australia is as a country. You get excited when you hit the Perth area as you are over Australia, but then realise you have another six hours to go to get to Sydney or Melbourne. You could take your international flight to Perth and spend a few days there first. It is a very different experience to the east coast of Australia.
  4. Schedule in some downtime. Australia has some of the most spectacular beaches in the world. Make sure you get to enjoy them. It is a long flight to get there and sightseeing can be tiring, so you deserve some time to relax and enjoy the beautiful coastline the country has to offer.Our favourites are the Whitsunday Islands in Queensland, Noosa in Queensland, Manly or any of the northern beaches in Sydney, and some of the beaches on the incredible Great Ocean Road between Melbourne and Adelaide. Of course, there are many, many, more, but these are beaches that are close to or on the way to popular sightseeing spots and worth looking at to see if they fit in with your itinerary.
  5. If you are looking to do some of the key tourist activities, make sure you book ahead. Walking over the Sydney Harbour Bridge, attending a show at the Opera House or an Aussie Rules game would be amazing experiences, but they get booked up well in advance. Don’t be disappointed. Get organised.

And, of course, you can buy Australian Dollars with Travel FX and enjoy the trusted, reliable, door to door service and excellent rates that you are used to for all of your overseas trips.
